questions.q: The Constituent Assembly of India was formed after the?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly of India was formed after the Quit India Movement in 1946.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly had members from which of the following groups?
opts: (B) Representatives from all major parties and groups
(D) Only Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly had members from various groups, including Congress, Muslim League, Scheduled Castes, and Scheduled Tribes.

questions.q: The Constituent Assembly's first meeting was held at?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly's first meeting was held at New Delhi on December 9, 1946.
questions.topic: First meeting of the Constituent Assembly

questions.q: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chairman of which committee?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee, which was responsible for drafting the Constitution.
questions.topic: Key Committees of the Constituent Assembly

questions.q: The Constitution's Preamble was written by?
opts: (D) Bhimrao Ramji Ambedkar and Jawaharlal Nehru
questions.explanation: The Preamble was written jointly by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: The Constitution of India was adopted on?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India was adopted on January 26, 1950.

questions.q: The first amendment to the Constitution was made in?
questions.explanation: The first amendment to the Constitution was made in 1952.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental right?
questions.explanation: Right to Property was not a fundamental right under the Constitution.

questions.q: The concept of 'Directive Principles of State Policy' was introduced by?
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Directive Principles of State Policy' was introduced jointly by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: The Constitution of India establishes a?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India establishes a federal system of government, with a parliamentary system at the center.

questions.q: The concept of 'Judicial Review' was introduced by?
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Judicial Review' was introduced by the Supreme Court of India.

questions.q: The Constitution of India has how many parts?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India has four parts: Parts I-IV.

questions.q: Which of the following is a feature of the Indian Constitution's parliamentary system?
opts: (A) The President has the power to appoint the Prime Minister
(B) The Prime Minister is the head of the government
(C) The Lok Sabha is the upper house of the Parliament
(D) The Lok Sabha is the lower house of the Parliament
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha is the lower house of the Parliament in the Indian parliamentary system.

questions.q: The Indian Constitution's Directive Principles of State Policy are inspired by?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution's Directive Principles of State Policy are inspired by the Irish Constitution.

questions.q: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was introduced by?
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was introduced by the Supreme Court of India.

questions.q: The Fundamental Duties of citizens are mentioned in?
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties of citizens are mentioned in Part IVA of the Constitution.

questions.q: The Constitution of India has how many amendments?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India has over 100 amendments.

questions.q: The concept of 'Federalism' in the Indian Constitution is based on?
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Federalism' in the Indian Constitution is based on the American Constitution.

questions.q: The Constitution of India grants special powers to the?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India grants special powers to the President.

questions.q: The concept of 'Parliamentary Supremacy' in the Indian Constitution means?
opts: (A) The Supreme Court has the power to make laws
(B) The Parliament has the power to make laws
(C) The President has the power to make laws
(D) The Judiciary has the power to make laws
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Parliamentary Supremacy' in the Indian Constitution means that the Parliament has the power to make laws.

questions.q: The Constitution of India is a living document because it?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India is a living document because it is flexible and adaptable.

questions.q: The concept of 'Separation of Powers' in the Indian Constitution means?
opts: (A) The power to make laws is divided among the three branches of government
(B) The power to enforce laws is divided among the three branches of government
(C) The power to interpret laws is divided among the three branches of government
(D) The power to make laws is concentrated in the hands of the President
questions.explanation: The concept of 'Separation of Powers' in the Indian Constitution means that the power to make laws is divided among the three branches of government.

questions.q: The Constitution of India grants special rights to the?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India grants special rights to various groups, including Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes, women and children, and minorities and backward classes.
